_doc: URLs of BloodHorse sale results/catalog pages. fetch_sales.py reads this on each run.
URL must end with a trailing slash; the scraper appends /{session} to walk all sessions.
kind: 'auto' is fine for everything — classify_entries() will route entries with prices
to recent-sale-results.json and entries without prices to upcoming-sales.json.
1. Browse https://www.bloodhorse.com/horse-racing/thoroughbred-sales/ to find the sale's results page
2. Copy the URL (everything before the /{session} number)
3. Add an entry below and run: python3 fetch_sales.py --verbose

sales.name: OBS Spring Sale of 2YOs in Training 2026

_examples_to_add_when_urls_are_known._comment: These need URL discovery from BH. Once you find a 2024 sale URL, paste it as 'url' and move from this list to 'sales' above.

_examples_to_add_when_urls_are_known.name_template: Keeneland September Yearling Sale 2026 (catalog when published)
